This whole exchange stemmed from Rain telling TMZ that she doesn’t believe Tay made $15 million in two weeks.
In August, Tay challenged Rain to get into the ring by calling her out in an Instagram video.
“Sophie Rain, $60 million is what I have for you to fight me in a boxing match.
“And don't pretend you're above it all just because you're scared I'm gonna beat the brakes off of you.
Tay’s beef with Rain seemingly stems from a TikTok shared by the Bop House account (which Rain co-founded) that featured Tay’s name in a list of young women who attended “auditions” to join the collective.
After it was released, Tay denied that she auditioned for the Bop House.
“Sophie Rain and Bop House, stop pretending like I auditioned to join the Bop House.
Why would I do that when I've already made more than all of y'all combined?” Tay said.
